target: Cross-WIN OTS canary boilerplate harmonization across WIN-035, WIN-036, WIN-037, WIN-038, WIN-039 plus reconciliation with the Timestamp Error tab and Part 1 / Part 6 / Part 8 prose. Updates the characterization of status_history.json contents to match the file as actually fetched 2026-05-06.

audit_finding.finding: Each per-domain entry in status_history.json bundles BOTH prediction-side fields (formula, predicted, tolerance_pct, source, sometimes falsification text) AND observation-side fields (observed, error_pct, pass, plus rigor sub-object). The two characterizations on our site disagree: the WIN-035 through WIN-039 detail_verdict_text boilerplate says the file 'bundles prediction parameters together with observation results' (broadly correct), while the Timestamp Error tab and Part 1 / Part 8 prose say the file contains 'observed values, pass/fail audit results, and statistical comparisons' only with predictions in monitor.py / docs/model.html (incorrect — those locations also hold the prediction-side fields, but the bundled file demonstrably carries them too). Both characterizations need to be unified around the file's actual contents.
cited_examples: Schumann Frequency: formula='f = 7.83 Hz (stable cavity)', predicted=7.83, observed=7.83, tolerance_pct=5, source='Tomsk SR / stable published'
NMP Longitude: formula='Decelerating toward Siberia >130°E', predicted=139.3, observed=138.057, source='NOAA NP.xy year=2025.0'
SAA Decay Rate (TTB): formula='B(r_SAA) exponential decay > 28 nT/yr', predicted=77, observed=77, source='INTERMAGNET TTB annual means (WIN-015)'
Lunar Magnetic Tide (M2): predicted='1.2 nT ±0.5 nT', observed='1.5', falsification='Fails if amplitude outside 0.7–1.7 nT for 7 consecutive days.'

canonical_boilerplate_html: OTS timestamps status_history.json, which per-domain bundles prediction-side fields (formula, predicted value, tolerance threshold, source label) together with observation-side fields (observed value, error percentage, pass/fail) at every commit. The structural prediction logic — the code that computes the predicted field — lives in mutable monitor.py source. The OTS hash proves the bundled file existed at the timestamp; it does not prove the prediction-side fields preceded the observation-side fields they are paired with. A genuine prospective timestamp would hash an isolated prediction document separately, before any confirming data is added.

patches.target_path: Timestamp Error tab scorecard 'What gets timestamped' card (~line 1822)
patches.find: status_history.json — observed values, pass/fail audit results, statistical comparisons. This is the answer sheet.
patches.replace: status_history.json — bundles prediction-side fields (formula, predicted value, tolerance) together with observation-side fields (observed value, pass/fail, error percentage) per domain per commit. The bundle is the answer sheet plus the question key on the same page; the OTS hash covers both — but cannot show one preceded the other.

patches.find: OpenTimestamps anchors status_history.json, the file containing reference data: observed values, pass/fail results, and statistical comparisons. These are observations — the outcomes. The prediction parameters — the formulas, expected values, and tolerances — live in monitor.py source code and docs/model.html, which are only git-versioned, not blockchain-timestamped.
patches.replace: OpenTimestamps anchors status_history.json, a file whose per-domain entries bundle prediction-side fields (formula, predicted value, tolerance threshold, source label) together with observation-side fields (observed value, error percentage, pass/fail) at every commit. Both sides land in the file simultaneously — the OTS hash covers the bundle, not a temporal ordering between the prediction text and the observation it is paired with. The structural prediction logic — the code that computes the predicted field — lives in monitor.py source code, with the static prediction text mirrored in docs/model.html; both are only git-versioned, not blockchain-timestamped.

holes_addressed: WIN-036.c3 hole 1 (MAJOR): cross-WIN OTS-canary inconsistency between WIN-035-039 boilerplate and Timestamp Error tab, both unifying around the audited contents of status_history.json

Internal coherence across data/wins.json (5 WINs), build-scripts/generate-html.js (Timestamp Error tab), and data/sections.json (part1, part6, part8) so a defender cannot compile our two own statements into a 'pick one' attack.
new_evidence_added: Live audit of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/data/status_history.json (587 entries, 13.7MB, fetched 2026-05-06T23:59:46Z) — confirms each per-domain entry contains formula + predicted + tolerance_pct + source AND observed + error_pct + pass; some entries also carry a falsification text and a rigor sub-object

Note that the URL referenced in the curmudgeon recommendation (/api/current/status_history.json) returned a GitHub Pages 404; the live file lives at /data/status_history.json. The expansion's audit uses the live path.

anticipated_objections.objection: The harmonized boilerplate is longer and may dilute the rhetorical force of the original 'bundles prediction parameters together with observation results' framing.
anticipated_objections.response: The added precision is what closes the cross-page inconsistency the curmudgeon flagged. The new wording specifies the field-level bundle (formula, predicted, tolerance vs observed, error_pct, pass) which is the audit-true claim — a defender pasting status_history.json next to our text will see one-to-one correspondence with our boilerplate. The cost is ~25 words per WIN; the benefit is rhetorical coherence across five WINs, the Timestamp Error tab, and three Part-prose locations.

anticipated_objections.objection: If the file already contains the prediction-side fields, the OTS critique weakens — the dome could argue OTS does anchor the prediction text after all.
anticipated_objections.response: It does anchor the prediction text — at the same commit as the observation. The point of our critique is unchanged: anchoring both sides of the ledger simultaneously gives no temporal precedence between them, which is what a prospective registration requires. The harmonized text now states this directly: 'The OTS hash covers the bundle, not a temporal ordering between the prediction-side and observation-side fields.' The dome would still need to anchor a predictions-only document before any data arrived to demonstrate temporal priority.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why not just pick the 'observations only' framing (matching the Timestamp Error tab) and reword the WIN boilerplate to match?
anticipated_objections.response: Because that framing is factually incorrect against the live file. status_history.json demonstrably contains formula, predicted, tolerance_pct, and source per domain per commit (audit-confirmed 2026-05-06). Aligning toward the wrong claim hands the dome defender a verifiable counter-example. The reverse — aligning toward 'bundles both' — matches the file's actual contents and is independently verifiable by anyone curl-ing the URL.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author's OpenTimestamps implementation is technically real and sound: the OTS infrastructure anchors a published file to the Bitcoin chain, and status_history.json IS a comprehensive per-timestamp audit artifact that records what the dome said its prediction was at each commit. For someone who already trusts the data, the file is genuinely useful: it preserves both the predicted value AND the observed value AND the formula AND the tolerance threshold as a tamper-evident record. The OTS layer adds a real temporal floor — the bundled file demonstrably existed by the timestamp. What it cannot do is establish temporal precedence between the prediction-side and observation-side fields within the bundle, because both got into the file at the same commit. The fix is structural (a separate predictions-only file, anchored before any data arrives), not rhetorical.
cross_win_consistency: WIN-031 was listed in the curmudgeon target list for this expansion but does NOT in fact contain the 'bundles prediction parameters together with observation results' boilerplate (verified by grep on data/wins.json detail_verdict_text fields 2026-05-07). Scope is therefore 5 WINs (035, 036, 037, 038, 039), not 6. WIN-031's detail_verdict_text references monitor.py separately and does not need a parallel patch.
scope_note: This expansion is mechanical text harmonization across 5 WINs, the Timestamp Error tab in generate-html.js, and 3 paragraphs in sections.json. It does NOT touch any verdict, code_analysis tag, or numerical claim. Tests should pass post-build with no data-blob changes — only prose. Curmudgeon's hole-2 (WIN-007 relabels_standard tag flip), hole-3 (WIN-036 detail_claim wording), hole-4 (batch members named explicitly), hole-5 (19.298° vs 19.3°) are separate items and out of scope for EXP-290.
